title: Distil this HR-tech acquisition memo to its go/no-go fundamentals
guidance: Reward the version that isolates the two or three things that actually determine the outcome. Penalise anything that restates market size without connecting it to the deal thesis.

runner_input.instruction: Distil the acquisition memo below to its go/no-go fundamentals for a partner pre-read.
runner_input.constraints: Max 200 words. State go, no-go, or conditional. Surface the deciding factors and the single biggest open question.

source_blocks.text: Proposed bolt-on: SyntheticHR Ltd, UK payroll-and-onboarding SaaS. £8m ARR; 18% YoY growth (decelerating from 34%). Asking price: £52m (6.5x ARR). Rationale: accelerates our expansion into SME payroll; plugs a product gap our current portfolio company has been feeling for 18 months.

items: NRR 94% — modest but acceptable for SME segment
Churn concentrated in accounts <10 seats (42% of churned logos last year)
Integration risk: different data model from acquirer's core platform; estimated 9-month migration
Acquirer's sales team has no SME payroll experience — would need new motion or partnership
Regulatory tailwind: UK HMRC real-time-information mandate drives urgency in target segment

rubric_notes: Integration risk (9-month migration) and GTM gap (no SME payroll experience) are the deciding factors.
Growth deceleration (34% to 18%) should be flagged as it undermines the bolt-on rationale.
The regulatory tailwind is real but does not offset the GTM gap — penalise responses that treat it as decisive.
